channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Students in Iran continue protests over 19-year-old’s murder on campus for second day
item.description: Student protesters in Iran called out officials at the University of Tehran for a second day on Saturday following the murder of a business student on campus.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump officials to begin peace talks with Russia in Saudi Arabia
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio, National Security Adviser Mike Waltz and Special Envoy to the Middle East Steve Witkoff are likely to meet with senior Russian officials in Saudi Arabia in the coming days to discuss an end to Russia's war in Ukraine, NewsNation reported on Saturday.
item.dc:creator: Ailia Zehra, The Hill

item.title: Rwanda-backed rebels advance and Congolese forces retreat from eastern Congo's second largest city
item.description: GOMA, Congo (AP) — Panic swept through eastern Congo’s second-largest city on Saturday as residents and soldiers fled by the thousands, scrambling to escape the looming advance of Rwanda-backed rebels.
item.dc:creator: JUSTIN KABUMBA, SAM METZ and RODNEY MUHUMUZA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A 2-year-old girl and her mother have died after the car-ramming attack in Munich
item.description: Police say a 2-year-old girl and her mother have died two days after they were injured in the car-ramming attack on a labor union demonstration in Munich.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Israel releases Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings, attempted murders
item.description: Palestinian prisoners linked to suicide bombings and attempted murders against Israelis are among the 369 released Saturday by Israel in exchange for hostages.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: American-Israeli hostage released by Hamas embraces his wife, family after being apart for nearly 500 days
item.description: American-Israeli hostage Sagui Dekel-Chen embraced his wife on Saturday, following his release from Hamas, after being apart from her for nearly 500 days.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: White South Africans gather in support of Trump and his claims that they are victims of racism
item.description: Some white South Africans have showed support for President Donald Trump and gathered at the U.S. Embassy in Pretoria to claim they are victims of racism by their own government.
item.dc:creator: Alfonso Nqunjana And Gerald Imray, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Hamas frees 3 more hostages in exchange for more than 300 prisoners as part of ceasefire deal with Israel
item.description: Hamas freed three more hostages on Saturday in exchange for more than 300 Palestinian prisoners as part of the fragile ceasefire agreement with Israel.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Kentucky bourbon bottles signed by the pope raise thousands for charity
item.description: It sounds like the opening line of a joke: What happens when the pope gets his hands on two bottles of Kentucky bourbon?
item.dc:creator: BRUCE SCHREINER and DYLAN LOVAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Idle buses, empty Border Patrol boats: Arrests for illegal crossings fall in the Rio Grande Valley
item.description: Border Patrol agents in the Rio Grande Valley have seen a sharp drop in apprehensions along the southern border since the new administration started.
item.dc:creator: Valerie Gonzalez, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Vance meets with leader of far-right German party
item.description: Vice President Vance met Friday with a leader of the far-right Alternative für Deutschland (AfD) party, making him the highest-ranking U.S. official to do so.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: Europe quietly works on a plan to send troops to Ukraine for post-war security
item.description: BRUSSELS (AP) — Increasingly alarmed that U.S. security priorities lie elsewhere, a group of European countries has been quietly working on a plan to send troops into Ukraine to help enforce any future peace settlement with Russia.
item.dc:creator: LORNE COOK, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hostages freed from Gaza discover personal lives shattered by Hamas
item.description: RAMAT GAN, Israel (AP) — After 484 days of captivity in Gaza, Keith Siegel had many questions. Was his 97-year-old mother still alive? Which of his neighbors was killed in Hamas' Oct.
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: German prosecutors investigate possible Islamic extremist motive for Munich car-ramming attack
item.description: MUNICH (AP) — The suspect in the car-ramming attack in Munich that injured 39 people appears to have had an Islamic extremist motive, but there's no evidence that he was involved with any radical network, authorities said Friday.
item.dc:creator: PHILIPP JENNE and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope has respiratory tract infection, is in fair condition and running mild fever, Vatican says
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis was hospitalized Friday after a weeklong bout of bronchitis worsened and was receiving drug therapy for a respiratory tract infection, the Vatican said.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Suspect in Munich car attack had 'Islamist motivation,' prosecutor says
item.description: The suspect accused of injuring more than 30 people in a car ramming attack in Munich appears to have been motivated by Islamic extremism, prosecutors say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Drone damages outer shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant; Russia denies responsibility
item.description: A drone armed with a warhead hit the outer protective shell of Ukraine's Chernobyl nuclear plant early Friday, damaging the structure and briefly starting a fire.
item.dc:creator: Illia Novikov, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pope Francis hospitalized for bronchitis treatment, Vatican says
item.description: Pope Francis was hospitalized on Friday as he suffers from an ongoing bout of bronchitis, the Vatican tells Fox News. He is also expected to undergo tests during his stay, according to a report.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Archaeologists unearth ruins of nearly 2,000-year-old Roman basilica in London
item.description: While exploring the site of a 32-story planned skyscraper in London, archaeologists unearthed the ruins of a nearly 2,000-year-old Roman basilica.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Armed fighters have raped scores of children in eastern Congo, UNICEF says
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — The U.N. children’s fund on Thursday accused armed men, likely on both sides of the conflict in eastern Congo, of raping scores of children over the past weeks as rebels expand their footprint and push government forces out.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: A driver plows into a demonstration in Munich, injuring 30 people
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — A driver drove a car into a labor union demonstration in central Munich on Thursday, injuring 30 people including children, authorities said. Officials said it was believed to be an attack. The suspect, an Afghan asylum-seeker, was arrested.
item.dc:creator: GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia rejoices at Trump-Putin call as Zelenskyy rejects talks without Ukraine present
item.description: Russian officials and state media took a triumphant tone Thursday after President Donald Trump jettisoned three years of U.S. policy and announced he would likely meet soon with Russian President Vladimir Putin to negotiate a peace deal in the almost three-year war in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: BARRY HATTON, ILLIA NOVIKOV and DASHA LITVINOVA, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian crypto expert Alexander Vinnik returns to Moscow in Russia-US prisoner swap, reports say
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Alexander Vinnik, a Russian cryptocurrency expert who faced Bitcoin fraud charges in the United States, returned to Russia on Thursday after being freed in a swap that saw Moscow release American Marc Fogel, Russian news agencies reported.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 4 dead, 26 hurt in Taiwan food court explosion
item.description: Four people were killed and another 26 were injured following a Thursday explosion at the Shin Kong Mitsukoshi department store in Taichung, Taiwan, according to authorities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Gas explosion at Taiwan food court kills 4 and injures 26
item.description: A gas explosion at a department store in Taiwan on Thursday killed four people and injured 26, fire authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Steel tariffs could disrupt international bridge expansion
item.description: The steel 2-lane Starr-Camargo International Bridge in Rio Grande City, Texas, is gearing up for an expansion of lanes, however they might not be steel after 25% tariffs were imposed by the Trump administration, the bridge's owner Sam Vale told Border Report on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Sandra Sanchez, WGN 9

item.title: Israel amassing troops in and around Gaza ahead of hostage deadline
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu warned that the Gaza ceasefire will end if Hamas does not release the remaining hostages this upcoming weekend.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Jack the Ripper's identity revealed after DNA match, historian says
item.description: A historian believes he has determined Jack the Ripper's identity through a DNA match of a shawl found at the scene of one of his murders.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: King Abdullah says Jordan will bring in 2K 'very ill' Palestinian children
item.description: Trump is not backing down from his plans for the future of Gaza and is pressuring Jordan, Egypt and other Arab countries to take in Palestinians.
item.dc:creator: Kellie Meyer, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says he may withhold aid to Jordan, Egypt if they don’t take in Palestinians
item.description: President Trump said Monday he would consider withholding aid to Jordan and Egypt if they do not agree to take in Palestinians as part of a plan for the U.S. to take over and develop the Gaza Strip.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Hamas' threat to delay hostage release raises fears for ceasefire
item.description: The next handover of three hostages had been scheduled for Saturday, and families say time is running out for those still alive.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Trump says ceasefire deal should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all hostages by Saturday
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump said Monday that a precarious ceasefire agreement between Israel and Hamas should be canceled if Hamas doesn’t release all the remaining hostages it is holding in Gaza by midday on Saturday — though he also said that such a decision would be up to Israel.
item.dc:creator: ZEKE MILLER and SAM MAGDY, Associated Press, WGN 9
